models.title: Models
models.intro: On this screen you can enable and disable individual LLM provider models, and set their capabilities and pricing.
models.sync.message: Loaded data for {providers_count} providers, new models: {models_new}
models.detail.article:
Adjust registry fields for the selected model: enable or disable it, set the context window and per-million token prices, and mark input/output modalities and extra features (chat, tools, structured output, reasoning).

Click Save to apply changes. Invalid numbers show a validation error.
models.detail.empty_banner: Select a model on the left to view and edit its settings.
project.discovery.article:
Place files to process in the project documents folder; open it with the Open documents button.

After adding files, click Refresh — the table lists files in supported formats.

Search scope: Root only — files directly in the docs folder; Nested folders — recursive subfolders. Markdown output follows the same folder layout.

Supported formats:
- documents: pdf, docx, odt
- text: txt, md
- images: png, jpg, jpeg, webp, bmp, gif, tif, tiff, svg

there is currently no support for: legacy Word (.doc); spreadsheets and tabular files (xls, csv, and similar); presentations (pptx and similar). RTF and other formats are also unsupported.
project.save_modal.title: Save
project.save_modal.validation_message: Fix the errors and save again.

project_execution.folder_modal.title: Folder
project_execution.folder_hint.docs_missi: The «docs» folder was not found. Add a docs directory to the project root.
project_execution.folder_hint.result_mis: The «result» folder was not found. It appears after the save-results step.
project_execution.run_modal.title: Run
project_execution.tagging.skip_mode: Skip mode: LLM is not called.
project_execution.tagging.no_llm_config: Tags were not created: set a provider and model in project settings.
project_execution.tagging.create_tags_of: «Tag documents» is off: LLM is not called.
project_extract.article:
The «Text extraction» section controls how document content is obtained.

A key difficulty with many formats is that it is unclear what is on images: it may be a text scan, a photo, or a diagram.

Most settings relate to image handling; if you prefer not to tune everything, the defaults work for many cases.

Things to watch:
- PDF render scale and image size limits (office documents, standalone images, including SVG after rasterization) strongly affect cost and recognition time; values much above ~1500 px are rarely worth it
- If your PDFs are scans, they often have both an image layer and a low-quality OCR text layer; adaptive mode or full-page render is usually better
- If office documents truly have no images, choose an algorithm that ignores images to save processing
project_extract.provider.markdown_extrac: Copies the document to the result folder without changing the body
Keeps the body (no LLM rewrite) but removes tags and YAML front matter (tags, description)
Plain text is sent to the LLM; the system rebuilds a new document
project_extract.provider.pdf_extract_pro: A PDF may hold real text, scans or photos, sometimes with an automatic OCR text layer. From experience on many documents, adaptive mode is the best default
Uses embedded text and ignores images; tries to normalize line order and reading order
Each page is rasterized and sent to vision/OCR models
Each page is scored: pages with images or low-quality text (garbled OCR) go to vision/OCR; others are extracted as text
Width of the page image when rasterized. Higher values improve text recognition but increase cost (token usage) and processing time
project_extract.provider.office_extract_: Uses document text and ignores images
Text is kept as extracted; all images are sent to vision/OCR
Text is kept as extracted; tiny images (e.g. logos) are skipped, larger ones go to vision/OCR
Maximum image size by long edge. Higher values improve text recognition but increase cost (token usage) and processing time (office documents and image files)
project_extract.provider.text_extract_pr: Reads the text available in the file
project_extract.provider.image_extract_p: Images are sent to vision/OCR models
Maximum image size by long edge. Higher values improve text recognition but increase cost (token usage) and processing time (office documents and image files)
project_image_processing.text_recognitio: Images from documents are ignored and are not saved to the output folder.
Uses multimodal LLMs with image support. They do not merely read text—they interpret it, so you get structured output and better handling of tables. There is still a risk of model hallucination.

Uses OCR (optical character recognition) models. They do not understand or analyze meaning—only the characters on the image. Tables are usually weaker, but they do not hallucinate or substitute values.
project_image_processing.yandex_key_bann: Yandex OCR is unavailable: the yandex API key is not set.
project_image_processing.ocr.intro: Only Yandex OCR is available at the moment.
project_image_processing.ocr.hint_model: OCR model in use; keeping «page» is recommended
project_image_processing.vision.hint_mod: Pick a multimodal LLM. Listed models have «Accepts images» enabled (see «Model settings» on the main screen)
project_image_processing.vision_model.re: Whether the model is allowed to reason before answering. Can improve recognition and typo correction. «Low» is recommended
project_image_processing.vision_model.te: Roughly the model's creativity; not every provider/model supports it. 0.0 means no creativity and strict output. 0.3 is recommended to give the model room to fix typos.
If you get an error about this parameter, set the value to 1.0
project_image_processing.vision_model.ex: The model receives an instruction to transcribe text from the image accurately; you can add details to steer its behavior.
project_image_processing.article:
## Text recognition
Text recognition method

Skip
> Images from documents are ignored and are not saved to the output folder.

Vision provider
> Uses multimodal LLMs with image support. They do not merely read text—they interpret it, so you get structured output and better handling of tables. There is still a risk of model hallucination.

OCR provider
> Uses OCR (optical character recognition) models. They do not understand or analyze meaning—only the characters on the image. Tables are usually weaker, but they do not hallucinate or substitute values.

## Provider and model
### Provider
Vision LLM API vendor

### Model
Pick a multimodal LLM. Listed models have «Accepts images» enabled (see «Model settings» on the main screen)

## Model tuning
### Reasoning
Whether the model is allowed to reason before answering. Can improve recognition and typo correction. «Low» is recommended

### Temperature
Roughly the model's creativity; not every provider/model supports it. 0.0 means no creativity and strict output. 0.3 is recommended to give the model room to fix typos.

> If you get an error about this parameter, set the value to 1.0

### Extra instructions
The model receives an instruction to transcribe text from the image accurately; you can add details to steer its behavior.

## Provider and model
Only Yandex OCR is available at the moment.

### Provider
OCR API vendor

### Model
OCR model in use; keeping «page» is recommended
project_markdown.mode_desc.none: Skip markup normalization and keep the text produced by extraction and recognition.
project_markdown.mode_desc.llm_processin: Send text to an LLM to normalize markup and edit content when needed.
project_markdown.hint.model: LLM model to use. The list shows models with “Accepts text” enabled (see «Model settings» on the main screen).
project_markdown.hint.reasoning: Whether the model may think before answering. Reduces hallucinations and errors; improves quality. «Low» is recommended.
project_markdown.hint.temperature: Roughly how creative the model is; not every provider or model supports it. 0.0 is fully deterministic. Try 0.5–0.9.
project_markdown.hint.temperature_quote: If you get an error about this parameter not being supported, set the value to 1.0.
project_markdown.hint.system_prompt: A strict target-Markdown instruction is always sent. Add guidance on normalization—drop boilerplate, promos, page numbers, shorten, or preserve structure.
project_markdown.article:
«Markdown creation» defines how raw text (after extraction and optional image processing) becomes the final Markdown.

«Skip» keeps extraction output without LLM normalization. LLM mode sends text to the selected model to normalize markup.

Providers and models depend on API keys. Models and «Accepts text» are configured under «Model settings» on the main screen.
project_pipeline.create_documents_index_: Create index.md with links to all project documents. When a short description or tags are available, they are added next to the link. The index makes it much easier to navigate files when working with agents.
project_pipeline.threads_section_descrip: The application uses caching and multi-threading at every document processing stage. More threads finish processing faster, but you may hit API provider rate limits.
project_pipeline.extract_threads_hint: Text extraction and rendering pages to images.
project_pipeline.image_processing_thread: Text recognition threads for Vision/OCR models. Very high values can cause request rate limit errors.
project_pipeline.yandex_ocr_banner:
When using Yandex OCR for images, only one
processing thread applies — RPS limits on the service side.
project_pipeline.markdown_threads_hint: Markup generation tasks via LLM models. Very high values can trigger rate-limit errors.
project_pipeline.tagging_threads_hint: Threads for document tagging and description generation. Used when the operating mode is "Batch" (Tagging section). Very high values can cause request rate limit errors.
project_pipeline.article:
Create document index — index.md in result lists linked documents; short descriptions and tags from metadata are included when present. Helps navigation when using agents.

Execution settings — caching and parallelism per stage; more threads speed things up but API limits may apply.

Text extraction threads — text extraction and page render to images.

Text recognition threads — for Vision/OCR; very high values risk rate limit errors (Yandex OCR — see the warning below the field).

Markup generation threads — LLM markup generation. Tagging threads — document tagging and description generation; only when "Batch" (Tagging section).

Discovery always runs in a single thread — there is no separate setting.
project_tagging.postprocessing_intro: At this step the model receives markdown content but does not edit it—it builds YAML document markup. Choose which fields to generate.
project_tagging.mode_desc.skip: No post-processing, tags, or short summary.
project_tagging.mode_desc.linear: Documents are processed one after another, without multithreading. The LLM always sees tags from earlier documents, so duplicate tags with conflicting meanings are rare.
project_tagging.mode_desc.parallel: In batch mode (thread count is set on the «Project» tab) processing is much faster, but the LLM may introduce synonym tags.
project_tagging.field.create_tags_hint: Ask the LLM to analyze the document and add tags to the markup. The model always receives tags from previously processed documents and your initial tag set.
project_tagging.field.tag_format_hint: Tags from the LLM are normalized to one format—pick the style you prefer.
project_tagging.field.description_hint: Ask the LLM to add a short document summary (1–2 sentences). Useful for the document index and navigation.
project_tagging.field.date_hint: Ask the LLM for an approximate document date when it appears in context (e.g. contract signing or lab date). Format YYYY-MM-DD.
project_tagging.hint.model: LLM model to use. The list shows models with “Accepts text” enabled (see «Model settings» on the main screen).
project_tagging.hint.reasoning: Whether the model may think before answering. Reduces hallucinations and errors; improves quality. «Low» is recommended.
project_tagging.hint.temperature: Roughly how creative the model is; not every provider or model supports it. 0.0 is fully deterministic. Try 0.5–0.9.
project_tagging.hint.temperature_quote: If you get an error about this parameter not being supported, set the value to 1.0.
project_tagging.hint.extra_instructions: A strict instruction for the extra fields you enabled is always sent. You can refine how the model writes descriptions or tags (e.g. forbid inventing new tags).
project_tagging.hint.start_tags: One tag per line or comma-separated—tell the LLM which tags you want in the document, or pair with instructions below to allow only these tags.
project_tagging.hint.start_tags_quote: Do not put system instructions in this field—only the initial tag list.
project_tagging.article:
«Tagging» runs after Markdown generation.

«Skip» turns off post-processing and related LLM options on this tab. Linear and batch modes use the selected provider and model.

Post-processing defines YAML fields (tags, short description, date). Tag format and initial tag set appear only when «Create tags» is on.

Batch mode is faster; thread count is on the «Project» tab.

Providers and models depend on API keys. Models and «Accepts text» are configured under «Model settings» on the main screen.
settings.title: Settings
settings.application.article:
Configure the interface language, application debug mode, and system log level.

The interface language is loaded from the app's locale files and stored in the configuration.

Debug mode adds providers for testing and writes more detailed logs; keep it off for normal use.

The log level controls which messages are written to the log file (DEBUG is the most verbose, CRITICAL only critical events).
settings.application.desc.language: GUI language; stored in the application configuration.
settings.application.desc.debug: Adds providers for testing the app and saves detailed logs about its operation.
settings.application.desc.log_level: Minimum level of messages written to the log file.
settings.llm.desc.lmstudio_api_key: Optional, but configuring it is recommended.
settings.llm.desc.gateway_connect_timeou: How long to wait while opening the TCP/TLS connection to the API host. Does not limit how long the model may take to stream a reply.
settings.llm.desc.gateway_read_timeout: How long to wait for the full HTTP response body after the connection is established. LLM calls often need several minutes; set this high enough.
settings.llm.article:
LLM providers are used in pipeline stages that need text models (for example Markdown generation or tagging).

If a provider offers multimodal models (they accept images), you can use them to recognize text in documents.

In the row labeled with the provider name, choose "Enabled" and enter the API key for each service you use. When "Disabled", key fields are hidden. Keys are stored in the application configuration on this machine.

Connect timeout applies only until the connection to the API is up; read timeout applies while the response is received. Long generations are limited by read timeout, not connect timeout.
settings.ocr.article:
Yandex OCR is used during image processing when the project uses OCR mode.

In the "Yandex OCR" row, choose "Enabled" and enter Key ID and Key Secret from Yandex Cloud. When "Disabled", the key block is hidden. Without valid keys, Yandex recognition will be unavailable.

Keys are stored locally in app.ini (or the linked configuration store).
settings.save_modal.title: Settings
